data.title: The U.S. and China have agreed to spin off TikTok's American operations
data.description:
The United States and China have shaken hands on a deal to divest the short-form video content app TikTok’s U.S. business to a consortium of mostly American investors led by Oracle (ORCL) and Silverlake, according to a report by Semafor on Thursday.

The deal is expected to close this week, the report said, and would result in the app's Beijing-based parent, ByteDance (BDNCE), having a stake just under 20%.

Oracle, Silverlake, and Abu Dhabi-based investment firm MGX will have a 15% stake each in TikTok USA (TIKTOK); other investors include Susquehanna, Dragoneer, and Michael Dell’s family office.

A report from Reuters in September said TikTok USA would be two companies. 

The first company would be the joint venture, which includes American investors and other parties who will own and handle U.S. user data and algorithms, in which ByteDance would be the single largest minority shareholder but will have no control over the U.S. version of the app.

The second company, which will be wholly owned by ByteDance, will control revenue-generating business operations such as e-commerce and advertising.

It was not immediately clear how much ByteDance received for TikTok's U.S. business, but Vice President JD Vance had previously said the deal would value the unit at roughly $14B.

data.title: Spirit Airlines in deal talks with Castlelake - CNBC
data.description:
Spirit Airlines (FLYYQ) is in talks with alternative investment firm Castlelake about a potential takeover as the discount airline looks to exit bankruptcy. 

It wasn't immediately clear if Spirit's bondholders and Castlelake would come to a deal or what form it may take, according to a  CNBC report on Thursday, which cited people familiar with the matter.

Spirit Airlines’ parent company, Spirit Aviation (FLYYQ) filed for bankruptcy protection for a second time in August.

Spirit and Castlelake didn’t immediately respond to CNBC requests for comment.

Frontier Airlines (ULCC) had been in recent talks with Spirit (FLYYQ) for a potential merger but wasn't able to secure a deal, according to the CNBC report.

data.title: Lam Research rises as Citi ups target on more wafer equipment upside
data.description:
• Lam Research (LRCX) shares rose 1.7% in premarket trading on Thursday after Citi upped its price target on the semiconductor equipment firm, citing the possibility for more wafer equipment spending.
  • “We believe the group remains in Phase 2 upcycle with 2026 [wafer fab equipment] likely tracking towards our bull case of $126B,” analyst Atif Malik wrote in a note to clients. “While LRCX stock has ran up 30% YTD following a string of positive industry updates, our [wafer fab equipment] sensitivity analysis shows further upside. We raise CY26/27 EPS by 4%/7% based on incremental [wafer fab equipment] upside, and lift TP to $265 on 40x P/E applied to CY27 EPS to reflect rising of AI sales contribution and group multiple re-rating.”
  • Lam Research is set to report quarterly results on Jan. 28. A consensus of analysts expects the company to earn $1.17 per share on $5.24B in revenue.

data.title: FTAI Aviation signs multi-year materials agreement with CFM International
data.description:
• FTAI Aviation (FTAI) on Thursday said it has signed a multi-year materials agreement with CFM International to provide component supply and repair support for CFM56 aircraft engines.
  • The agreement gives FTAI access to OEM replacement parts, thrust performance upgrades and component repair services for the global CFM56 engine fleet, the companies said.
  • CFM International is a joint venture between GE Aerospace and Safran Aircraft Engines.
  • FTAIN closed +0.14% at $25.51.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: POET Technologies prices $150M registered direct offering of shares
data.description:
POET Technologies (POET) on Thursday announced the pricing of a $150M worth of registered direct offering of shares.

The company has entered into securities purchase agreements with institutional investors for the purchase and sale of 20,689,656 shares of common stock.

Shares were -3.11% pre-market to $8.11.

The offering is expected to close on or about January 23, 2026.

Net proceeds are intended to be used for corporate development, including targeted acquisitions, scaling up of R&D, acceleration of its high-speed optical module and light source businesses, expanding operations, and general working capital.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Freeport to phase in Grasberg block cave restart in Q2, issues outlook
Datadog rises as Stifel upgrades on 'attractive' risk-reward
data.description:
• Datadog (DDOG) shares rose 3.4% in premarket trading on Thursday after investment firm Stifel upgraded the technology company, citing an “attractive” risk-reward profile.
  • “We are upgrading Datadog to Buy as recent checks lead us to believe the company will post another larger than typical quarterly (~4%+) beat in 4Q driven by accelerating core growth, continued strong gains within the non-OAI genAI cohort and Q/Q OAI stability,” analyst Brad Reback wrote in a note to clients. “The net result is we believe DDOG's 4Q growth rate, excluding OAI, should be [more than] 23%.”
  • Reback, who raised his rating to Buy from Hold, but cut his price target to $160 from $205, added that the company is seeing an acceleration in its core business, citing recent commentary from Datadog management at an investor conference.
  • “Further, while still early, we believe that newer products (CloudPrem, DEX, Security, Service Management, Bits AI), a broadening of the genAI customer cohort, as well as newer GTM initiatives around Enterprise, Channel and International, should begin to supplement Datadog's existing revenue base as they gain scale in coming quarters/years,” Reback added.

data.title: Mingteng to execute 1-for-200 reverse stock split
data.description:
Mingteng International (MTEN) will execute a reverse stock split of its ordinary shares on a 1-for-200 basis.

The company’s Class A ordinary shares will begin trading on a post-split basis when the market opens on January 26, 2026.

Upon the effectiveness, every 200 shares of the company’s issued and outstanding Class A ordinary shares as of the effective date will automatically be combined into 1 Class A ordinary share, and every 200 shares of its Class B ordinary shares will be combined into 1 Class B ordinary share.

Such adjustments will reduce the total number of outstanding Class A ordinary shares from around 242.33M to ~1.21M and the total number of outstanding Class B ordinary shares will be reduced from ~2M to ~10,455.

MTEN -22% premarket to $0.023

Source: Press Release
